ai_summary.summary: Failed to fetch Cluster API v1.11.11 manifest due to GitHub API 504 timeout

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test suite failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to fetch the Cluster API v1.11.11 core components YAML manifest from GitHub. The failure was caused by a 504 Gateway Timeout error when trying to access https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml. This is evidenced by the error message in artifacts/junit.e2e_suite.1.xml: 'failed to get component source YAML from URL: [timed out waiting for the condition, failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504]'. The build log at line 1379 confirms this with the same error. This is a transient infrastructure issue related to GitHub's API availability or network connectivity rather than a bug in the CAPZ codebase.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is due to a transient network/GitHub API issue (504 timeout) when fetching a manifest. The available evidence (logs and test output) confirms this is an external infrastructure problem. No code changes are required. The test should be retried when the GitHub API is available.

The error was 'timed out waiting for the condition' with a 'got status code 504' response, as shown in build-log.txt at line 1379. This indicates a transient network or GitHub availability issue rather than a code defect in the CAPZ project itself. The 504 Gateway Timeout error and timeout condition clearly point to an infrastructure-level problem during the test setup phase. The junit.e2e_suite.1.xml file confirms this was a setup phase failure occurring in SynchronizedBeforeSuite before any test cases ran.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is due to a transient network/GitHub availability issue during manifest download. The specific evidence is a 504 status code and timeout when fetching https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml. To resolve, simply retry the test run as this is an infrastructure flake.

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to inability to fetch cluster-api v1.11.11 manifests from GitHub

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed during the setup phase when trying to create a local clusterctl repository. The failure occurred while attempting to fetch the core-components.yaml manifest for cluster-api v1.11.11 from GitHub (line 1379 in build-log.txt). The error message indicates 'timed out waiting for the condition' and 'got status code 504' when trying to access https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml. This is a transient infrastructure issue related to network connectivity or GitHub availability rather than a bug in the CAPZ codebase. The junit XML file confirms this is a SynchronizedBeforeSuite failure occurring at test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go:64.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once the upstream service recovers; no CAPZ-side change required. The failure is due to a transient network/GitHub issue (504 status code when fetching external manifest).

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to a 504 Gateway Timeout when fetching CAPI v1.11.11 manifests from GitHub during clusterctl repository initialization.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test suite failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to fetch Cluster API manifests for version v1.11.11 from GitHub. The error message 'failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504' from build-log.txt line 1389 and junit.e2e_suite.1.xml indicates a 504 Gateway Timeout from GitHub's servers. This is a transient infrastructure issue related to network connectivity or GitHub availability rather than a bug in the CAPZ codebase. The failure occurred before any cluster provisioning or CAPZ-specific logic could execute.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure is a transient network/GitHub issue that resolved on its own. The evidence shows a 504 Gateway Timeout from GitHub when fetching CAPI manifests, which is an external infrastructure problem. No code changes are required. To prevent this class of issue in future runs, consider mirroring critical external dependencies or implementing retry logic with exponential backoff for manifest downloads.

The error message from build-log.txt line 1379 and junit.e2e_suite.1.xml shows 'failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504'. This indicates a transient network issue or GitHub availability problem rather than a code defect in CAPZ. The failure occurred before any cluster was provisioned, as confirmed by the empty results from discover_clusters and discover_controllers. The error was preceded by a 'timed out waiting for the condition' message, suggesting the system waited for the URL to become available but eventually received the 504 error.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure was caused by an external network/GitHub availability issue during manifest fetching. The only concrete evidence is the 504 error from GitHub's servers when trying to download the CAPI v1.11.11 release manifest. A retry of the test job should resolve this transient infrastructure flake.

ai_summary.summary: SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed due to a 504 Gateway Timeout when fetching CAPI v1.11.11 core-components.yaml from GitHub during clusterctl repository initialization.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test suite failed during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase when attempting to fetch the Cluster API v1.11.11 core-components.yaml manifest from GitHub. The error message from build-log.txt line 1379 and junit.e2e_suite.1.xml shows 'failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.11.11/core-components.yaml: got status code 504'. This indicates a transient network issue or GitHub availability problem rather than a code defect in CAPZ. The failure occurred before any cluster was provisioned, as confirmed by the empty results from discover_clusters and discover_controllers.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: The failure was caused by an external network/GitHub availability issue during manifest fetching. The only concrete evidence is the 504 error from GitHub's servers when trying to download the CAPI v1.11.11 release manifest. A retry of the test job should resolve this transient infrastructure flake.
